question: For the branch-current runtime-adjacent surfaces identified in the runtime truth inventory, what should be treated as keep-now runtime, keep-as-support, keep-but-off, retained legacy compatibility, maybe-later archive territory, or still-needs-verification?

classification_rules.keep_now_runtime: Observed on the dominant branch-current runtime spine or runtime SSOT path.
classification_rules.keep_runtime_support: Not the primary observed runtime authority, but still mode-gated, support-relevant, or directly adjacent to active execution.
classification_rules.keep_optional_off: Declared surface exists but is currently null or disabled on the active runtime snapshot.
classification_rules.keep_legacy_compatibility: Explicitly retained compatibility/test-only surface; not branch-current runtime SSOT.
classification_rules.needs_verification: Present in code/helpers but not observed strongly enough on the active runtime spine to justify lifecycle action.
classification_rules.archive_now: Not established by this slice; archive workflow requires reference/provenance checks.

keep_optional_off: other disabled research override surfaces on the active runtime snapshot

keep_legacy_compatibility.reason: File header says legacy compatibility shim; internal runtime should import features_asof SSOT instead.
File header says intentionally legacy/test-only and must not be treated as runtime-config authority.

needs_verification.reason: Observed in vectorized helper code, but not strongly observed on the dominant runtime spine.
Present in helpers/indicator implementations, but not proven as a dominant active runtime decision path in this slice.
Present as helper territory, but not proven as a dominant current decision input.

key_observations: evaluate.py imports extract_features_backtest/extract_features_live directly and comments that deprecated wrappers should be avoided in core runtime code.
evaluate.py still keeps a compatibility wrapper around core.strategy.regime for shadow-observer/test-parity behavior, so regime.py is support/mode-gated rather than archive-ready.
DEAD_GHOST_ZOMBIE audit already treats features.py and validator.py conservatively as compatibility/test-only style surfaces rather than active runtime SSOT.
GENESIS_TOPOLOGY_WORK_QUEUE says archive work requires its own provenance/reference checks, so this slice cannot honestly classify any runtime-adjacent code surface as archive-now.

recommended_cleanup_pilot.name: Pilot 01 — legacy containment
recommended_cleanup_pilot.goal: Keep compatibility surfaces visible but clearly demoted from SSOT/runtime status.

unverified: Whether core.strategy.regime is materially exercised in paper/live paths outside the inspected spine.
Whether MACD/helper surfaces become active in symbol/timeframe-specific paths not sampled here.
Whether external consumers still rely on the compatibility shim or legacy validator.
Whether a later provenance-checked archive workflow would clear any of these surfaces.
what_changed: The runtime inventory now has an explicit disposition map.
A smallest admissible cleanup pilot is named.
No archive-now surfaces were established in this slice.
what_did_not_change: No files were moved, archived, or deleted.
Unrelated local modifications remained out of scope.
